content:
# FastAPI Best Practices

## Project Structure
```
app/
  api/
    v1/
      endpoints/
      router.py
  core/
    config.py
    security.py
    database.py
  models/
  schemas/
  services/
  main.py
```

## API Design
1. Use proper HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
2. Return appropriate status codes
3. Use Pydantic for request/response validation
4. Implement proper error handling

## Security
1. Use OAuth2 with JWT tokens
2. Hash passwords with bcrypt
3. Validate all inputs
4. Use CORS properly
5. Rate limit endpoints

## Database
1. Use async SQLAlchemy for better performance
2. Implement connection pooling
3. Use migrations (Alembic)
4. Add proper indexes

## Code Example:
```python
# Good: Async endpoint with proper error handling
@router.get("/{id}", response_model=ItemResponse)
async def get_item(
    id: str,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)
):
    item = await service.get_by_id(id)
    if not item:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Item not found")
    if item.owner_id != current_user.id:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Not authorized")
    return item
```

# Tailwind CSS Best Practices ## Organization 1. Use consistent class ordering 2. Extract common patterns to components 3. Use @apply sparingly 4. Configure custom colors in tailwind.config.js ## Responsive Design 1. Mobile-first approach 2. Use breakpoint prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:) 3. Test on real devices ## Class Order Convention: 1. Layout (flex, grid, position) 2. Spacing (margin, padding) 3. Sizing (width, height) 4. Typography (font, text) 5. Colors (bg, text color) 6. Borders 7. Effects (shadow, opacity) 8. Transitions ## Code Example: ```tsx // Good: Organized classes
Content
```
